ace: noun. A playing card showing a single pip, typically the highest or lowest ranking card in a game.
acre: noun. An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day 's ploughing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840
agar: noun. A gelatinous substance obtained from red algae, especially Gracilaria species, used as a bacterial culture medium, in electrophoresis
agaric: noun. Any of various fungi, principally of the order Agaricales, having fruiting bodies consisting of umbrella -like caps, on stalks, with
age: noun. The amount of time that some being has been alive, or that some thing has been in existence, as measured from its birth or origin
ager: noun. One who or that which ages something.
air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
airer: noun. A framework upon which laundry is aired; a clotheshorse.
arc: noun. That part of a circle which a heavenly body appears to pass through as it moves above and below the horizon.
are: verb. second-person singular simple present of be
area: noun. A measure of the extent of a surface; it is measured in square units.
areca: noun. Any member of the genus Areca of about fifty species of single-stemmed palms in the family Arecaceae, found in humid tropical forests.
areic: adjective. Of or pertaining to area; especially used to describe a measurement per unit area.
aria: noun. A musical piece written typically for a solo voice with orchestral accompaniment in an opera or cantata.
cage: noun. An enclosure made of spaced bars, normally to hold animals while providing some ventilation.
cager: noun. A person or machine responsible for managing a mineshaft cage.
cagier: adjective. comparative form of cagey: more cagey
car: noun. A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for
carer: noun. Someone who regularly looks after another person, either as a job or often through family responsibilities.
carr: noun. Any marsh; marshy ground, swampland.
carriage: noun. The act of conveying; carrying.
cigar: noun. A cylinder of tobacco rolled and wrapped with an outer covering of tobacco leaves, intended to be smoked.
cire: noun. A fabric with a glazed finish.
crag: noun. A rocky outcrop; a rugged steep cliff or rock.
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
era: noun. A time period of indeterminate length, generally more than one year.
erg: noun. A unit of work or energy, being the amount of work done by a force of one dyne applied through a distance of one centimeter.
erica: noun. Any of many heathers, of the genus Erica, used as garden plants
gar: noun. Any of several North American fish of the family Lepisosteidae that have long, narrow jaws.
gear: noun. Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
race: noun. A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective.
racer: noun. Agent noun of race: one that races or is used for racing.
racier: adjective. comparative form of racy: more racy
raga: noun. Any of various melodic forms used in Indian classical music, or a piece of music composed in such a form.
ragi: noun. A grain, of species Eleusine coracana, cultivated as a cereal in arid areas of Africa and Asia; finger millet.
raia: noun. Alternative form of raya (“type of historical territory”).
rear: verb. To bring up to maturity, as offspring; to educate; to instruct; to foster.
rerig: verb. To rig again; to outfit (a ship) with new rigging.
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rice: noun. Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.
ricer: noun. A person, especially a Native American, who cultivates and harvests rice.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
acer: noun. A plant of the genus Acer; a maple.
acerra: noun. In Ancient Rome, a small box for holding incense.
aiger: noun. Alternative form of eagre; a tidal bore.
ara: noun. The great blue and yellow macaw (Ara ararauna).
arace: verb. To tear up by the roots; to draw away.
arg: noun. An argument; a value passed as a parameter.
carga: noun. A traditional Spanish unit of dry measure, equivalent to about 222 liters.
crare: noun. A slow unwieldy trading vessel.
eir: determiner. Belonging to em, gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, equivalent to the singular their and coordinate
eric: noun. A fine paid as compensation for violent crimes.
garce: noun. A unit of volume or weight for rice, etc.
gare: noun. Coarse wool on the legs of sheep.
gari: noun. Alternative form of garri (“West African food made from cassava”).
gracer: noun. Agent noun of grace: one who graces.
grice: noun. A pig, especially a young pig, or its meat; sometimes specifically, a breed of pig or boar native to north Britain, now extinct.
gricer: noun. A railway enthusiast, a trainspotter.
grr: interjection. Sound of an animal growl.
